title: Mine the correct tree among multiple objects
ambiguity: There are two visible trees, and language alone does not specify oak versus birch.
temporal_context: Previous frame showed the cursor moving toward the left tree.
why_visual_prompt_helps: The mask fixes the spatial target while the interaction type says to mine rather than approach or use.

title: Approach the furnace instead of the crafting table
language_instruction: Go to the station and use it.
ambiguity: Both furnace and crafting table are valid stations, but only the furnace supports smelting.
temporal_context: Previous plan step was smelt iron ore, so the station should be the furnace.
why_visual_prompt_helps: The mask selects the furnace, and the interaction type indicates right-click/use instead of mining.

title: Switch target from stone to iron ore
ambiguity: The previous target was stone, so a policy may continue mining the old block if context is not updated.
temporal_context: Previous mask was on stone; current mask moves to iron ore.
why_visual_prompt_helps: The temporal mask transition communicates target switching more directly than a short language update.

title: Avoid mining the wrong block
ambiguity: Coal, iron, and diamond are all useful, but the intended subgoal is iron for tool progression.
temporal_context: Current plan says obtain iron ingot before returning to diamond.
why_visual_prompt_helps: The mask prevents a visually nearby but strategically wrong target from being selected.

title: Use a shelter door instead of breaking a wall
ambiguity: A language-only policy may break a wall or path to the wrong cell instead of using the door.
temporal_context: The agent is outside at night and should preserve the shelter structure.
why_visual_prompt_helps: The door mask and use interaction encode both the correct object and the correct affordance.
